what_it_does: Declares a single RELATION_TYPE dict for a 'same_law' board edge: a directed conceptual edge asserting that two addressed perspectives/claims express the same underlying law/principle seen in different places, surfaced during the wind-down harvest.
observations: Module-level constant RELATION_TYPE only; no functions or classes.
Fields: id, directed (True), label ('same-law'), desc; no inverse field.
desc frames it as the 'pattern-unification edge' between addressed perspectives/claims (the harvest's RELATIONS).
desc states the edge is first-class on the board so the linking layer parses it rather than collapsing it to 'references'.
desc says it is surfaced by-use during the wind-down harvest (recollection).
standouts: No inverse declared, despite directed=True (differs from reply_to in this batch).
Explicitly resists collapse into a generic 'references' edge.
conventions: directed=True without a stated inverse is a deviation from the directional-edge-with-inverse law seen in reply_to.
concerns: Marked directed=True but declares no inverse; a same-law relation reads as symmetric, so directionality and the missing inverse are unexplained from this file alone.
notes: A conceptual pattern-unification edge that says two claims are the same law; kept first-class so it isn't flattened to 'references'.
questions: Why is this edge directed=True with no inverse when 'same law' appears symmetric?
What is the 'wind-down harvest / recollection' process that surfaces these edges?
purpose_vs_actual: Match: the file declares exactly the same-law conceptual edge its purpose states.
apparent_themes: Preserving conceptual relations rather than flattening them
intention_signals: Aims to give the board a dedicated edge for 'these two things are the same principle' so the recollection harvest can record pattern-unification as a distinct, parseable relation.
novelty: A first-class 'same underlying law/principle' edge whose existence is justified by refusing to collapse it into generic references.
contribution: Contributes the same-law pattern-unification edge definition that lets harvested perspectives/claims be linked as instances of one principle.
summary_for_embedding: A board_edges relation-type declaration defining the directed same_law edge that links two addressed perspectives or claims as the same underlying law/principle, kept first-class for the linking layer.
intention_for_embedding: To record pattern-unification across the corpus as a distinct board edge surfaced during the recollection harvest, instead of collapsing it into a generic reference.
